message.content:
I'm planning implementation for an interview skeleton project at c:\Users\Basyc\Desktop\test\interview-ai-fullstack-v1.

Explore Module C (BullMQ jobs) and Module D (WebSocket). Read these files fully and report their exact current stub content, the Chinese TODO doc-comments, and what needs implementing:
- src/services/jobService.js
- workers/jobWorker.js
- src/models/job.model.js
- src/routes/v1/jobs.route.js
- src/controllers/ (job controller if exists)
- src/validations/ (job validation if exists)
- src/lib/eventBus.js
- src/lib/wsServer.js
- src/index.js (how WS server and worker are wired)
- src/app.js
- frontend/pages/dashboard.jsx
- frontend/lib or api helpers used by dashboard/login
- src/routes/v1/index.js (registered routes)

For each stub, quote the TODO comment and describe required behavior. Note the 4-phase pipeline (preprocess→transform→build→package), eventBus event naming (job:${jobId}), and how progress is emitted/forwarded. Report file paths with line numbers. Return only conclusions needed to write an implementation plan.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver
- document-skills:xlsx
- document-skills:docx
- document-skills:pptx
- document-skills:pdf
- frontend-design:frontend-design
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review
- security-review

11	 * - status: String, enum ['queued', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed'], default 'queued'

13	 * - createdAt: Date, default Date.now
14	 * - completedAt: Date
15	 */

17	  // TODO: 实现 schema 字段
18	});
19	
20	// add plugin that converts mongoose to json
21	jobSchema.plugin(toJSON);
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Job
25	 */
26	const Job = mongoose.model('Job', jobSchema);
27	
28	module.exports = Job;

1	// eslint-disable-next-line no-unused-vars
2	const httpStatus = require('http-status');
3	// eslint-disable-next-line no-unused-vars
4	const ApiError = require('../utils/ApiError');
5	
6	/**
7	 * Middleware: extract tenantId from JWT claim and attach to req.tenantId
8	 *
9	 * 候选人实现要求：
10	 * 1. 从 req.user.tenantId 读取 tenantId（JWT 已由 Passport 验证，req.user 由 jwtStrategy 填充）
11	 * 2. 若 tenantId 缺失，调用 next(new ApiError(httpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED, 'Tenant required'))
12	 * 3. 否则设置 req.tenantId = req.user.tenantId 并调用 next()
13	 *
14	 * ⚠️ 禁止从 req.body / req.query / req.headers['x-tenant-id'] 取值
15	 */
16	const requireTenant = (req, res, next) => {
17	  // TODO: 实现租户提取逻辑
18	  next();
19	};
20	
21	module.exports = requireTenant;
22

content.text:
I have gathered everything needed. Here are my findings for Module C (BullMQ jobs) and Module D (WebSocket).

## Module C — BullMQ Jobs

### `src/services/jobService.js` (55 lines)
- Queue infrastructure already wired: `getQueue()` (lines 9-16) lazily creates `new Queue('job-pipeline', { connection: { url: config.redis.url } })`. `JOB_QUEUE_NAME = 'job-pipeline'` (line 6). Exports `{ submit, getJob, JOB_QUEUE_NAME, getQueue }`.
- **`submit(tenantId, payload)` — line 33-36.** TODO line 34: `// TODO: 实现完整的任务提交流程`. Currently `return { jobId: 'NOT_IMPLEMENTED' }`. Doc (lines 18-31) requires: (1) call `billingService.deduct(tenantId)`; on failure throw `ApiError(402, 'Insufficient balance')`; (2) `jobId = uuidv4()`; (3) `getQueue().add(jobId, { jobId, tenantId, phases: PHASES })`; (4) create Mongo `Job` doc `{ jobId, tenantId, status: 'queued', phases: [] }`; (5) `return { jobId }`. Note: `uuidv4`, `billingService`, `Job` model, and `ApiError` are NOT yet imported here — must be added. `PHASES` referenced in doc lives in `workers/jobWorker.js`.
- **`getJob(jobId, tenantId)` — line 50-53.** TODO line 51: `// TODO: 实现带租户隔离的 Job 查询`. Currently `return null`. Doc (lines 38-48): query Mongo `Job` with BOTH `{ jobId, tenantId }`; return null if not found / tenant mismatch.

### `workers/jobWorker.js` (57 lines)

## Module D — WebSocket

### `src/lib/eventBus.js` (7 lines)
- Complete, no TODO. A singleton Node `EventEmitter` with `setMaxListeners(100)` (line 4). This is the bridge: worker emits `job:${jobId}`, WS server listens on `job:${jobId}`. No changes needed.

### `src/lib/wsServer.js` (39 lines)
- **`attachWsServer(server)` — line 24-37.** TODO line 25: `// TODO: 实现 WebSocket 服务端`. Currently `void`s everything and `return null`. Imports available: `WebSocket` (ws), `jwt`, `config`, `eventBus`. Doc (lines 6-23) requires: (1) create `new WebSocket.Server({ server, path: '/ws/job' })` — but actual client URL is `ws://host/ws/job/:jobId?token=<jwt>`, so routing/jobId must be parsed from `request.url` (path option won't match the `/:jobId` suffix, so likely use `noServer`/manual upgrade or parse URL in `connection`); (2) on `connection`: (a) parse `request.url` for `jobId` and `token` query param; (b) `jwt.verify(token, config.jwt.secret)`; (c) query Mongo to confirm `job.tenantId === tokenPayload.tenantId` (tenant isolation); (d) on failure `ws.close(4001, 'Unauthorized')`; (e) on success register an `eventBus` listener for `job:${jobId}` that does `ws.send(JSON.stringify(event))`; (f) when an event has `progress === 100`, close connection after 500ms delay; (3) on client disconnect (`close`), remove the eventBus listener to prevent leaks. Note: needs the `Job` model imported for the tenant check (not currently imported). `jwt.secret` comes from config.

### `src/index.js` (49 lines) — wiring
- Wiring is already complete and does NOT need changes: after `app.listen` (line 19), it calls `attachWsServer(server)` (line 24, passing the HTTP server) and `startWorker()` (line 28). MongoDB connected (line 12) and `billingService.seed(tenants)` run first (line 16). So WS + worker share the same process and the in-memory `eventBus` singleton — critical: worker and WS server must be in the same process for `eventBus` to bridge them (they are).

### `src/app.js` (67 lines)
- Standard koa-style express boilerplate. Mounts `/v1` routes (line 54), passport jwt init (lines 45-46), 404 + error middleware (lines 57-65). No TODO, no changes needed for these modules.

### `src/routes/v1/index.js` (49 lines)
- Registered default routes: `/auth`, `/users`, `/billing`, `/jobs` (lines 11-28). `/docs` only in development (lines 30-36). `jobsRoute` and `billingRoute` already imported and mounted — no changes needed.

### Frontend API helpers / lib
- **No `frontend/lib`, no shared API/fetch helper exists.** Only pages are `_app.jsx`, `dashboard.jsx`, `login.jsx`. All HTTP/WS calls are done inline with raw `fetch`/`WebSocket` against `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL` and `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL`. No axios wrapper to reuse — each page implements its own calls.

## Cross-cutting dependencies (blocking, also stubbed)
These are prerequisites the job/WS flow depends on and are also unimplemented:
- **`src/middlewares/requireTenant.js` (line 16-19)** — TODO line 17. Currently just `next()` with no `req.tenantId` set. Must set `req.tenantId = req.user.tenantId` (from JWT claim only; must NOT read body/query/`x-tenant-id` header) and 401 `'Tenant required'` if missing. Both `/jobs` and `/billing` routes depend on this producing `req.tenantId`.
- **`src/services/billingService.js` (58 lines)** — `deduct` (line 24, returns `NOT_IMPLEMENTED`), `getBalance` (line 38, returns 0), `seed` (line 52). All TODO. `jobService.submit` depends on `deduct` returning `{ ok, balance?, reason? }`; dashboard balance depends on `getBalance`. Uses Redis key `billing:${tenantId}`, Lua atomic deduct, `COST_PER_JOB = 10`.

## Key implementation-plan notes
- **Pipeline:** 4 sequential phases `preprocess→transform→build→package`, 3000ms each (~12s total per job), progress steps 25/50/75/100.
- **Event bridge:** worker `eventBus.emit(\`job:${jobId}\`, event)` → WS server `eventBus.on(\`job:${jobId}\`)` → `ws.send(JSON.stringify(event))`. Same event object shape flows all the way to `dashboard.connectWs`. Worker and WS server run in one process (`src/index.js`) so the in-memory eventBus works.
- **Tenant isolation enforced at three layers:** `requireTenant` (JWT claim only), `jobService.getJob` query `{ jobId, tenantId }`, and WS server verifying `job.tenantId === tokenPayload.tenantId` before streaming.
- **WS auth:** token passed as query param (`?token=`), verified with `jwt.verify(token, config.jwt.secret)`; unauthorized → `ws.close(4001)`; auto-close 500ms after `progress===100`; must deregister eventBus listener on disconnect to avoid leaks.
- **Missing imports to add:** `Job` model into `jobService.js`, `workers/jobWorker.js`, and `src/lib/wsServer.js`; `uuidv4`/`billingService`/`ApiError` usage in `jobService.submit`; `jobService` into `jobs.route.js`.
